description: This plot displays lnσ as a function of lnε for Fe–20Mn–6Al-0.6C-0.15Si austenitic low-density steel tested at three different temperatures to derive work hardening exponents, with -196 °C marked by blue triangles, -77 °C marked by red circles, and room temperature (RT) marked by grey squares. Each curve presents two separate work hardening stages with distinct n values: n₁=0.204, n₂=0.448 for -196 °C; n₁=0.181, n₂=0.439 for -77 °C; n₁=0.174, n₂=0.362 for RT. Both n values at each stage rise with decreasing test temperature, demonstrating that lower temperature brings improved work hardening capacity, which supports the superior tensile ductility of the steel under cryogenic conditions.
